We then made some major advancements with devices like the Oculus Rift, which gave users the ability to move around in virtual worlds and interact with their surroundings. A huge step forward, though these solutions had to remain plugged in. #VR https://t.co/nJ14RsKzTs

We first saw moving images with audio in a readily available consumer device when @Google launched Cardboard, a simple #VR “headset” made from — you guessed it — cardboard folded neatly to house a smartphone. Turns out paper products have played a big part in VR! https://t.co/93OY6OpX2K

In 2012, we got traction on the first dedicated #VR headset for public consumption: the Oculus Rift DK1. The goal of the @Kickstarter campaign was to get a prototype in the hands of game devs. The rest is history. (More on that history in a second…) https://t.co/S4jWOieaG8

Does anyone else remember @Disney Quest? It debuted in 1998, and was the first of its kind—a massive (100,000Sq ft!) indoor interactive theme park, with plenty of #VR elements. Sadly, it closed in 2016. But certainly has a place in the timeline of VR lore. https://t.co/1UuUqsZJfY

We can’t talk about #VR without mentioning @Nintendo’s groundbreaking Virtual Boy. Introduced in 1995, this quirky gaming machine was something of a hybrid between a console and a head worn VR device, using parallax graphics to create the illusion of depth. Major nostalgia! https://t.co/LqsvKXQD1u

The earliest version of a #VR-adjacent device is the View-Master. I remember seeing these as a kid (maybe I’m dating myself a bit), which use the sun to backlight family photographs and cartoons on film. https://t.co/v4n76HeaMG

Did you know #VR existed almost 200 years ago? This clip is fascinating and made me want to share some thoughts about how VR evolved from an optical illusion housed inside an accordion-like paper apparatus to the headsets like the @Meta Quest Pro that we use today. A quick 🧵 https://t.co/O4cRIux8pd

Listen to #TeamQualcomm CFO Akash Palkhiwala on The @SixFivePodcast with @danielnewmanUV discussing our FY22 Q4 earnings call 👇 

Key takeaways: Strong YOY growth; record Q4 revenues; our tech is at the heart of digital transformation across industries. https://t.co/NM0L4JLeiA
You can find our full FY22 Q4 earnings webcast and materials here. https://t.co/XFlsSqApU2

We remain on track to expand our addressable market by more than 7x to approximately $700B in the next decade and firmly establish @Qualcomm as the connected processor company for the intelligent edge.

Despite the current near-term headwinds for our industry, the fundamentals of our growth strategy remain intact. We are confident in our ability to navigate these challenges given our strong balance sheet, leading technology roadmap and deep customer relationships.
We also announced several key auto partnerships in FY22. @GWMGlobal, @MercedesBenz, @BMWGroup — just to name a few. @Qualcomm has established itself as a key technology partner for next-gen vehicles.

In auto, our design-win pipeline is now in excess of $30B. The acquisition of @ArriverSW positions us as a leader in #ADAS, while the @Snapdragon #DigitalChassis is the preferred platform for next-gen vehicles.

In handsets, we entered a new multi-year agreement with our great partner @SamsungMobile—@Snapdragon platforms will power premium #Galaxy products globally for years to come. We also secured a number of premium and high-tier design wins with Chinese OEMs.
Now, I’d like to take a minute to focus on some important wins over the course of FY22.
All of this demonstrates our efforts to transition @Qualcomm from a wireless comms company for the mobile industry to a connected processor company for the connected intelligent edge are working.

In Q4 FY22 alone, we delivered record revenues of $11.4B and non-GAAP earnings per share of $3.13—reflective of record chipset business and solid performance in licensing. QCT revenues ($9.9B) were up 28% YOY—including record performance in auto and #IoT.
Our total FY22 non-GAAP revenues of $44B were up 32% YOY. QCT—the growth engine of the company—contributed $37.7B of that total, including combined automotive and #IoT revenue growth of 38% YOY.

FY22 was strong for #TeamQualcomm, including record performance in our chipset business, sharp execution of our diversification and growth strategies (automotive, #IoT, etc.), big partnership announcements, and more. Some highlights from of FY22 Q4 earnings call 👇

At @Qualcomm, we’re proud and excited to work with global automakers like @renaultgroup and the broader ecosystem to enable the digital transformation of the automotive industry.

Cloud-connected cars will enable new services, e.g. allow automakers to track the health of a car over its lifespan & provide valuable real time service notifications. As cars begin to connect to infrastructure & other vehicles, transportation intelligence & safety will improve.

The @Snapdragon #DigitalChassis gives automakers and drivers the ability to customize their cars in ways that weren’t possible before — from infotainment and assisted driving solutions to services and applications and more.

.@Qualcomm’s role in automotive has been defined by our ability to connect everyone to everything, and build low-power, high-performance compute platforms. As the digital components in automobiles continued to increase, we saw an opportunity to build an entire #DigitalChassis.

The car is becoming a connected computer on wheels and car companies are becoming tech companies. As @LucaDe_Meo said: By 2030, software will represent 40% of a car’s value (30% more than today), and software defined vehicles will be a game changer for consumers and automakers.

Virtually all industries are in transition, we’re all working through how the future of the enterprise will evolve. This creates exciting challenges to solve, making it a great time to be in tech, developing solutions for what is quickly becoming an intelligently connected world.

These trends and advancements are reshaping the way we live and work. And, as hybrid work becomes the norm – even more than today – it’s another example of the merging of physical and digital spaces, which will help drive the evolution and expansion of the #metaverse.

Connectivity is everything. If a device isn’t connected in the future, it will be the exception. On-device intelligence is equally important. We’re just scratching the surface with #AI, but it’s already enabling extreme leaps in computational power & data processing at the edge.

To start, it’s extremely clear that digital transformation is taking place across all industries, enterprises and economies. Devices are becoming more intelligent and connected, and more data is being created outside traditional data hubs and being processed at the edge.

.@Qualcomm isn’t a communications company serving just mobile — it’s an industry leading connected processor company for the intelligent edge, serving multiple new end-markets and driving growth of the cloud economy. [9/9]

Our one technology roadmap across wireless connectivity, edge processing, and power-efficient #AI is unmatched in our peer group. Advanced processing and AI are in fact the fastest growing silicon content areas for @Qualcomm. [8/9]
These results demonstrate that our growth drivers remain unchanged, and proof that our diversification strategy is working. [7/9]

It was also an impressive quarter for our automotive business. Our automotive design win pipeline—bolstered by partnerships with leading automakers like @Stellantis—is now over $16 billion. That’s more than $3 billion in growth since Q1. [6/9]

Our chipset business—our growth engine—generated revenues of $9.5 billion. Our #IoT business grew 61 percent year-over-year, while RF front-end revenues grew 28 percent year-over-year and handset revenues grew 56 percent in that same period. [5/9]

And, these industry forces are accelerating, driving our strong financial performance and >7X TAM expansion to ~$700B in the next decade. In Q2 FY22 we delivered record revenues of $11.2 billion & non-GAAP earnings of $3.21 per share. [4/9]

These trends include enterprise transformation of the home, merging of mobile w/ PC, immersive experiences that blend physical and digital realities, connectivity & processing at the edge, connected cars, the #DigitalChassis, broad digital transformation across industries. [3/9]

As I said at Investor Day in November 2021, @Qualcomm is at the intersection of transformative trends; there is demand for our technology across virtually every industry. This has resulted in strong growth and diversification beyond smartphones. [2/9]

And, cars are becoming mini data hubs - they’ll generate between 5-15 gigabits of data per second—data that will need to be processed at the edge. It’s all made possible by #DigitalChassis apps like our cellular-vehicle-to-everything (#CV2X) solutions & Snapdragon Ride Platform.

The #DigitalChassis is a comprehensive set of automotive platforms for telematics, connectivity, computing, driver assistance and autonomy. It enables our partners in the auto industry to deliver connected, intelligent experiences that are safer, customizable, & immersive.

This is just the beginning of @Qualcomm’s automotive journey. We look forward to innovating with the industry for years to come — and to revolutionizing the world of transportation more broadly. 
8/8

This new era of intelligent transportation — powered by @Snapdragon tech — can drive the reduction of fuel consumption. And it helps that @Qualcomm’s history lies in making smartphones where battery life must last all day. Minimizing power consumption is in our DNA. 
7/8

Suddenly, because of innovations made possible by tech like our @Snapdragon Digital Chassis, we’re entering a new reality — one with smarter and safer transportation. 
6/8

So, what does that mean for the future of transportation? As cars become more connected to the cloud (thanks to #5G), they’ll become more connected to one another, as well as bikes, pedestrians, and infrastructure, such as traffic lights and road signs. 
5/8

By taking the same approach we did with mobile — following our One Technology Roadmap — we created a platform that’s scalable, for entry-level to luxury cars. This approach also worked for our automotive partners who, like us, view the car as a connected computer on wheels. 
4/8

The @Snapdragon Digital Chassis redefines the car experience. By incorporating ADAS, telematics, and connecting the car to the cloud, the car is transformed into a mobile data hub, improving safety, and allowing for highly personalized experiences. 
3/8

For years, people have used their phones for GPS, entertainment & communication within their cars. Early on, @Qualcomm understood that the car itself is a mobile device. We saw an opportunity for innovation and were determined to integrate those capabilities into the car. 
2/8

#TeamQualcomm worked hard on these technologies, which take advantage of more than a decade of groundbreaking #AI R&D in mobile-RF systems. Together, they enable #WhatsNextIn5G, including the leap to 10 Gbps peak speeds, improved performance, and better power efficiency.

4️⃣ Qualcomm Smart Transmit 2.0. 
A system-level technology that takes advantage of modem-to-antenna system awareness to increase upload data speeds and enhance coverage for #mmWave and Sub-6, while continuing to meet RF emissions requirements.

3️⃣ Qualcomm AI-Enhanced Signal Boost

@PatrickMoorhead It a long list. The 8CX Gen 2 is designed for the commercial segment. Key ones are Windows secure core PC compliant with full hypervisor support, improved camera / audio for better teams / zoom experience, and AI capabilities including eye correction during conferencing.

@anshelsag @Qualcomm Great question. We expect to see prices ranging from $125 to $250 USD. Lots of phones 😉. In China, 5G price points can already address 60% of the market. With the new Snapdragon 4 in Q121, the transition from 4G to 5G will significantly accelerate globally.

@geoffblaber If you believe that IT, enterprise data and office workloads are moving to the cloud, as well as the connection of physical and digital spaces, you believe in 5G enterprise private networks with a 5G enabled ACPC!😎 (2/2).

@geoffblaber 5G private networks is a significant opportunity. Not only for the industrial, smart cities, retail, etc. use cases already under development. It will be an essential part of the future enterprise alongside WiFi 6 (1/2).

@PatrickMoorhead mmWave requires new cell stations for a more dense network deployment. That takes time and the site permit process is municipality-by-municipality in many places. The new mid-band and DSS will bring broad coverage as mmWave expands. Peace, love and mmWave 👊(2/2)

@PatrickMoorhead Pat, we are driving mmWave aggressively to all Snapdragon tiers. We need mmWave for the full potential of 5G. We expect to see mmWave in most developed economies by 2021. China considering mmWave for the 22 Winter Olympics can be a game-changer for mass market devices (1/2).
